starting_state.K: Reader sees multiple documentation options but lacks a boundary for choosing.
starting_state.P: Reader defaults to balance instead of a source-of-truth decision rule.

target_state.K: Reader understands why repo-local docs are the stronger system of record.
target_state.P: Reader can choose based on version control and discoverability.

displacement.K: Shift from generic tradeoff framing to a source-of-truth distinction.
displacement.P: Shift from list-making to recommendation logic.
displacement.A: Shift from caution to owned decision.

layers.assert: Acknowledge that both Notion and Markdown are familiar options.
layers.exception: Break the balance by naming the source-of-truth requirement.
layers.log: Leave a repeatable rule for future documentation choices.

modules.name: old-belief-index
modules.interface_hook: The real question is what system can be trusted as the source of truth.

modules.name: expectation-break
logic: Show why familiarity is not the same as reliability.
modules.output_state: Need for a decision rule is visible
modules.interface_hook: That rule should favor versioned docs.

modules.name: new-framework
logic: Recommend the repository for source-of-truth docs.
modules.interface_hook: The remaining question is how to keep collaboration light.

modules.name: boundary-and-timing-anchor
logic: Separate source-of-truth docs from lighter collaboration surfaces.
modules.interface_hook: With that split, the recommendation feels practical instead of rigid.

modules.name: identity-embed
logic: End with a rule leadership can repeat.

spec.objective: Turn a balanced memo into a recommendation with source-of-truth logic.

diagnose.main_issue: The memo names tradeoffs but avoids making a decision.

rewrite.central_claim: The handbook and runbooks should live in the repository because they need version control and a durable source of truth.
edits: Use source-of-truth and discoverability as the reasoning spine.
Keep Notion as a secondary collaboration surface rather than the system of record.

remaining_risks: Could acknowledge one stronger tradeoff if expanded into a longer memo.

notes: The recommendation is distinguished from simple option listing.
A usable decision rule is explicit.
The memo sounds owned rather than evasive.
Decision logic survives across the whole short memo.
